source_pr.title: store/tikv: implement a `ttlManager` to update the TTL of a transaction
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

In the pessimistic transaction and the large transaction, we should update the TTL of the lock.

### What is changed and how it works?

Implement a `ttlManager` and use it in the pessimistic lock.
The `ttlManager` will spawn a goroutine to update the TTL of the primary lock periodically.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test


Side effects

 - Increased code complexity

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

 - Write release note for bug-fix or new feature.

root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is caused by a real data race around background TTL keepalive: the `ttlManager` runs a goroutine and used `CommitMaxBackoff`, which unit tests may update concurrently (a known source of `-race` reports). The PR stabilizes it by avoiding the shared/mutable global backoff in the goroutine and switching to a dedicated pessimistic-lock backoff constant, eliminating the racy access pattern.
fix_pattern: Avoid reading/writing shared mutable globals from background goroutines in tests; pass immutable values into goroutines (or use dedicated constants) to prevent data races under `-race`.

evidence: files: go.mod, go.sum, metrics/metrics.go, metrics/tikvclient.go, session/pessimistic_test.go, store/mockstore/mocktikv/mock_tikv_test.go, store/mockstore/mocktikv/mvcc.go, store/mockstore/mocktikv/mvcc_leveldb.go, store/mockstore/mocktikv/rpc.go, store/tikv/2pc.go, store/tikv/2pc_test.go, store/tikv/lock_test.go, store/tikv/region_request_test.go, store/tikv/tikvrpc/tikvrpc.go, store/tikv/txn.go, tools/check/go.mod
